2605.30227.title: Unifying Temporal and Structural Credit Assignment in LLM-Based Multi-Agent Prompt Optimization
2605.30227.paper_summary: While Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) empower Large Language Models to tackle complex reasoning tasks through collaborative interaction, optimizing their dynamics remains a formidable challenge due to the discrete, non-differentiable nature of the computation graph and the sparsity of global supervisory signals. Existing black-box optimizers struggle to attribute trajectory-level failure to specific local components, resulting in inefficient, high-variance exploration. We argue that tractable MAS optimization needs structural inductive biases to disentangle error signals. We propose temporal and structural credit assignment, which decomposes the objective along two axes: (i) temporal credit, using state-space bottlenecks to identify critical rounds, and (ii) structural credit, using stationary role policies to isolate agent contributions. Leveraging these decomposed signals, we introduce a discrete, verbalized block coordinate descent algorithm for iterative refinement. Rather than indiscriminate global updates, it alternates between optimizing role prompts and aggregation protocols, using LLM-generated "proxy gradients" to target only the identified weak links. Across diverse reasoning benchmarks, our approach substantially reduces query complexity while improving performance, providing a principled and interpretable path toward self-improving MAS.
2605.30227.paper_summary_zh: 雖然多智能體系統（MAS）使大型語言模型能夠通過協作互動來應對複雜的推理任務，但由於計算圖的離散性和不可微性以及全球監督信號的稀疏性，優化其動態仍然是一個艱巨的挑戰。現有的黑箱優化器難以將軌跡級別的失敗歸因於特定的局部組件，導致低效且高變異的探索。我們認為，可處理的MAS優化需要結構性歸納偏見來解開錯誤信號。我們提出了時間和結構的信貸分配，這沿著兩個軸解構目標：（i）時間信貸，利用狀態空間瓶頸來識別關鍵回合，以及（ii）結構信貸，利用靜態角色政策來隔離智能體貢獻。利用這些解構的信號，我們引入了一種離散的、口頭化的塊坐標下降算法進行迭代精煉。它不是進行不加區別的全局更新，而是在優化角色提示和聚合協議之間交替進行，使用LLM生成的“代理梯度”僅針對識別出的弱環節。在各種推理基準測試中，我們的方法顯著降低了查詢複雜性，同時提高了性能，為自我改進的MAS提供了一條原則性和可解釋的道路。
2605.30227.author: Wenwu Li et.al.
2605.30227.authors: Wenwu Li, Yuran Song, Mingze Zhao, Bo Jin, Wenhao Li
